patch.value:
Governance expectations:
- The release work is traceable from a parent epic to each release-readiness concern.
- Each item has owner, reviewer, risk, confidence, sprint, release, files/docs/tests, and acceptance criteria.
- Items are closed only after the related evidence exists.
- pm validate is run at the end and warnings are reviewed.
Document pm governance for the package lifecycle
Maintain a complete pm hierarchy for release readiness, including epics, features, tasks, issues, chores, linked artifacts, acceptance criteria, tests, and closure evidence.

- pm list-all shows a complete hierarchy for release work.
- pm validate reports no blocking metadata, lifecycle, resolution, command-reference, or history drift problems for package items.
- Closure notes summarize real evidence instead of generic done statements.
- Changelog generation consumes the closed pm items.
Created and maintained a strict pm hierarchy for release readiness with parent epic, features, task, issue, chore items, linked artifacts, acceptance criteria, evidence, and command references.
pm governance records are complete, traceable, and pass strict validation for package release work.
pm validate core checks passed with ok=true across metadata, resolution, lifecycle, command references, and history drift for 7 items; no warnings returned for those checks.
pm governance hierarchy and strict core validation are complete for the release-readiness work.
